DoS Hulk.threat_summary: A high-volume HTTP GET flood attack targeting the web server, sending thousands of requests per second to exhaust server resources and cause denial of service.
DoS Hulk.recommended_action: Block source IP at perimeter firewall. Enable rate limiting on load balancer. Contact ISP for upstream filtering if attack persists.

DoS GoldenEye.threat_summary: GoldenEye exploits HTTP keep-alive connections to consume all available server threads, preventing legitimate users from connecting.
DoS GoldenEye.recommended_action: Limit concurrent HTTP connections per IP. Configure connection timeout thresholds. Deploy WAF with connection rate rules.

DoS Slowhttptest.threat_summary: Slowhttptest sends incomplete HTTP requests with abnormally long headers to exhaust server connection pools over time.
DoS Slowhttptest.recommended_action: Set minimum header transmission rate. Configure request timeout. Update HTTP inspection rules to detect slow header patterns.

DoS slowloris.threat_summary: Slowloris holds connections open by sending partial HTTP requests, exhausting the server connection pool and blocking legitimate traffic.
DoS slowloris.recommended_action: Implement connection timeout limits. Use reverse proxy with connection queuing. Apply rate limiting per source IP.

Heartbleed.threat_summary: Exploitation of CVE-2014-0160 in OpenSSL heartbeat extension. Attacker reads up to 64 KB of server memory per request, potentially exposing private keys and credentials.
Heartbleed.recommended_action: Immediately patch OpenSSL to >= 1.0.1g. Revoke and reissue all SSL/TLS certificates. Rotate all private keys. Invalidate active sessions.

DoS Hulk.recommended_action: Block the source IP address 172.16.0.1 and investigate the network traffic

DoS GoldenEye.recommended_action: Block the source IP (172.16.0.1) and investigate the network for potential vulnerabilities

DoS Slowhttptest.recommended_action: Block the source IP address 172.16.0.1 and investigate the potential vulnerability

DoS slowloris.threat_summary: Slowloris attack detected, attempting to consume server resources
DoS slowloris.recommended_action: Block the source IP (172.16.0.1) and investigate the attacker's intent

Heartbleed.recommended_action: Immediately patch vulnerable systems and reissue certificates

DoS Hulk.threat_summary: A DoS Hulk attack is a type of denial-of-service attack that aims to overwhelm a target server by sending a large number of TCP requests, often with the reset flag set.
DoS Hulk.recommended_action: Implement rate limiting on the server, block the source IP, and monitor network traffic for similar attacks.

DoS GoldenEye.threat_summary: The DoS GoldenEye attack is a type of denial-of-service attack that targets web servers by exploiting the TCP protocol. It sends a large number of requests with manipulated timestamps to overwhelm the server, causing it to become unresponsive.
DoS GoldenEye.recommended_action: Implement rate limiting on the web server, monitor network traffic for unusual patterns, and consider deploying a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter out malicious traffic.

DoS Slowhttptest.threat_summary: A Slowhttptest attack is a type of Denial of Service (DoS) attack that targets web servers by sending slow and malformed HTTP requests, causing the server to consume excessive resources and become unresponsive.
DoS Slowhttptest.recommended_action: Implement rate limiting on incoming HTTP requests, monitor server performance, and consider deploying a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter out malicious traffic.

DoS slowloris.threat_summary: A denial-of-service attack that aims to keep many connections to a web server open and consume its resources, making it unavailable to legitimate users.
DoS slowloris.recommended_action: Implement rate limiting on the web server, monitor network traffic for unusual patterns, and consider deploying a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter out malicious traffic.

Heartbleed.threat_summary: A confirmed Heartbleed attack has been detected, exposing sensitive data and potentially allowing an attacker to gain unauthorized access to the affected system.
Heartbleed.recommended_action: Immediately patch all affected systems with the latest security updates, and consider reissuing SSL/TLS certificates to prevent further exploitation.
